---
title: "Visualization"
author: "Bailey Binando, Lillian Pates"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r package_load}
#| echo: false

# load packages
library(tidyverse)
library(sf) # vector data
library(terra) # rastor data
library(plotly)
library(here)
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r data_load}
#| echo: false

# load data

# floodplain shapefiles (2yr, 5yr, 10yr, 25yr, 50yr, 100yr)
floodplain_2yr  <- st_read(here("data", "processed", "floodplain_2yr.gpkg"),  quiet = TRUE)
floodplain_5yr  <- st_read(here("data", "processed", "floodplain_5yr.gpkg"),  quiet = TRUE)
floodplain_10yr <- st_read(here("data", "processed", "floodplain_10yr.gpkg"), quiet = TRUE)
floodplain_25yr <- st_read(here("data", "processed", "floodplain_25yr.gpkg"), quiet = TRUE)
floodplain_50yr <- st_read(here("data", "processed", "floodplain_50yr.gpkg"), quiet = TRUE)
floodplain_100yr <- st_read(here("data", "processed", "floodplain_100yr.gpkg"), quiet = TRUE)

# floodplain asset exposure
floodplain_exposure <- read_csv(here("outputs", "tables", "floodplain_exposure_summary.csv"))

poza_rica <- st_read(here("data", "processed", "poza_rica.gpkg"), quiet = TRUE)

# poza rica building footprint
buildings <- st_read(here("data", "processed", "buildings_clipped.gpkg"), quiet = TRUE)

# poza rica total assets
poza_rica_assets <- read_csv(here("outputs", "tables", "poza_rica_asset_summary.csv"))

# building exposure/vulnerability
exposure_vulnerability <- st_read(here("data", "processed", "building_exposure_vulnerability.gpkg"), quiet = TRUE)

# dem
dem <- rast(here("data", "processed", "dem_pozarica.tif"))

# river
river <- st_read(here("data", "processed", "river_clipped.gpkg"), quiet = TRUE)

# flood timeseries
flood_flow <- read_csv(here("data", "processed", "flood_timeseries_clean.csv"))

```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

```{r}

# 100yr floodplain
ratio_buildings_fp100 <- floodplain_exposure|>
  filter(floodplain == "fp100") |>
  pull(buildings_exposed_ratio)

pct_buildings_fp100 <- round(ratio_buildings_fp100 * 100, 2)

ratio_area_fp100 <- floodplain_exposure|>
  filter(floodplain == "fp100") |>
  pull(building_area_exposed_ratio)

pct_area_fp100 <- round(ratio_area_fp100 * 100, 2)

# 50yr floodplain
ratio_buildings_fp50 <- floodplain_exposure|>
  filter(floodplain == "fp50") |>
  pull(buildings_exposed_ratio)

pct_buildings_fp50 <- round(ratio_buildings_fp50 * 100, 2)

ratio_area_fp50 <- floodplain_exposure|>
  filter(floodplain == "fp50") |>
  pull(building_area_exposed_ratio)

pct_area_fp50 <- round(ratio_area_fp50 * 100, 2)

# 25yr floodplain
ratio_buildings_fp25 <- floodplain_exposure|>
  filter(floodplain == "fp25") |>
  pull(buildings_exposed_ratio)

pct_buildings_fp25 <- round(ratio_buildings_fp25 * 100, 2)

ratio_area_fp25 <- floodplain_exposure|>
  filter(floodplain == "fp25") |>
  pull(building_area_exposed_ratio)

pct_area_fp25 <- round(ratio_area_fp25 * 100, 2)


# 10yr floodplain
ratio_buildings_fp10 <- floodplain_exposure|>
  filter(floodplain == "fp10") |>
  pull(buildings_exposed_ratio)

pct_buildings_fp10 <- round(ratio_buildings_fp10 * 100, 2)

ratio_area_fp10 <- floodplain_exposure|>
  filter(floodplain == "fp10") |>
  pull(building_area_exposed_ratio)

pct_area_fp10 <- round(ratio_area_fp10 * 100, 2)


# 5yr floodplain
ratio_buildings_fp5 <- floodplain_exposure|>
  filter(floodplain == "fp5") |>
  pull(buildings_exposed_ratio)

pct_buildings_fp5 <- round(ratio_buildings_fp5 * 100, 2)

ratio_area_fp5 <- floodplain_exposure|>
  filter(floodplain == "fp5") |>
  pull(building_area_exposed_ratio)

pct_area_fp5 <- round(ratio_area_fp5 * 100, 2)

# 2yr floodplain
ratio_buildings_fp2 <- floodplain_exposure|>
  filter(floodplain == "fp2") |>
  pull(buildings_exposed_ratio)

pct_buildings_fp2 <- round(ratio_buildings_fp2 * 100, 2)

ratio_area_fp2 <- floodplain_exposure|>
  filter(floodplain == "fp2") |>
  pull(building_area_exposed_ratio)

pct_area_fp2 <- round(ratio_area_fp2 * 100, 2)
```

```{r}
#| label: floodplain_exposure_map
#| out-width: "100%"
#| fig-height: 6


library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(viridis)

cols <- viridis(7, option = "plasma", direction = -1)

map <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = poza_rica, 
          aes(text = paste0("Municipal Boundary",
                            "<br>Population: 189,457 (2020 Census)")), fill = "#f7f3ea", color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.2) +
  
  geom_sf(data = floodplain_100yr, 
          aes(fill = "Floodplain (100yr)",
              text = paste0("100-Year Floodplain",
                            "<br>Buildings Exposed: ", pct_buildings_fp100, "%",
                            "<br>Building Area Exposed: ", pct_area_fp100, "%")),
          alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  
  geom_sf(data = floodplain_50yr, 
          aes(fill = "Floodplain (50yr)",
              text = paste0("50-Year Floodplain",
                            "<br>Buildings Exposed: ", pct_buildings_fp50, "%",
                            "<br>Building Area Exposed: ", pct_area_fp50, "%")),
          alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  
  geom_sf(data = floodplain_25yr, 
          aes(fill = "Floodplain (25yr)",
              text = paste0("25-Year Floodplain",
                            "<br>Buildings Exposed: ", pct_buildings_fp25, "%",
                            "<br>Building Area Exposed: ", pct_area_fp25, "%")),
          alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  
  geom_sf(data = floodplain_10yr, 
          aes(fill = "Floodplain (10yr)",
              text = paste0("10-Year Floodplain",
                            "<br>Buildings Exposed: ", pct_buildings_fp10, "%",
                            "<br>Building Area Exposed: ", pct_area_fp10, "%")),
          alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  
  geom_sf(data = floodplain_5yr, 
          aes(fill = "Floodplain (5yr)",
              text = paste0("5-Year Floodplain",
                            "<br>Buildings Exposed: ", pct_buildings_fp5, "%",
                            "<br>Building Area Exposed: ", pct_area_fp5, "%")),
          alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  
  geom_sf(data = floodplain_2yr, 
          aes(fill = "Floodplain (2yr)",
              text = paste0("2-Year Floodplain",
                            "<br>Buildings Exposed: ", pct_buildings_fp2, "%",
                            "<br>Building Area Exposed: ", pct_area_fp2, "%")),
          alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  
  geom_sf(data = buildings, color = "grey40", alpha = 0.7, size = 0.06) +
  geom_sf(data = river, color = "#2b6cb0",
          aes(text = "Río Cazones"),
          linewidth = 0.75) +
  labs(
    title = "Floodplain Exposure",
    subtitle = "Words",
    fill = "Flood Extent"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Floodplain (2yr)" = cols[2],
      "Floodplain (5yr)" = cols[3],
      "Floodplain (10yr)" = cols[4],
      "Floodplain (25yr)" = cols[5],
      "Floodplain (50yr)" = cols[6],
      "Floodplain (100yr)" = cols[7]
    )
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  coord_sf(
  xlim = c(661970, 666857),
  ylim = c(2266165, 2278800),
  expand = FALSE
) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )

ggplotly(map, tooltip = "text")



```

```{r}
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 7
#| eval: FALSE
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(viridis)
library(sf)


#bbox <- st_bbox(buildings)
#bbox
#pad_x <- 
#pad_x <- (bbox["xmax"] - bbox["xmin"]) * 0.03
#pad_y <- (bbox["ymax"] - bbox["ymin"]) * 0.03
  
bbox <- st_bbox(buildings)

xmid <- (bbox["xmin"] + bbox["xmax"]) / 2 #- 0.03
ymid <- (bbox["ymin"] + bbox["ymax"]) / 2

zoom_factor <- 0.35   # smaller = more zoomed in

x_half <- (bbox["xmax"] - bbox["xmin"]) * zoom_factor / 2
y_half <- (bbox["ymax"] - bbox["ymin"]) * zoom_factor / 2


# -----------------------------
# Floodplain colors
# -----------------------------
#cols <- viridis(5, option = "mako", direction = -1)
library(RColorBrewer)
cols <- c("#084594", "#2171b5", "#4292c6", "#6baed6", "#9ecae1", "#c6dbef")

# -----------------------------
# Build ggplot map
# -----------------------------
map <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(
    data = floodplain_100yr,
    aes(fill = "Floodplain (100yr)"),
    alpha = 0.25,
    #color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_sf(
    data = floodplain_50yr,
    aes(fill = "Floodplain (50yr)"),
    alpha = 0.35,
    #color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_sf(
    data = floodplain_25yr,
    aes(fill = "Floodplain (25yr)"),
    alpha = 0.35,
    #color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_sf(
    data = floodplain_10yr,
    aes(fill = "Floodplain (10yr)"),
    alpha = 0.25,
    #color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_sf(
    data = floodplain_5yr,
    aes(fill = "Floodplain (5yr)"),
    alpha = 0.25,
    #color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_sf(
    data = buildings,
    fill = "grey75",
    color = "grey60",
    alpha = 0.75
  ) +
  geom_sf(
    data = river,
    color = "darkblue",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  coord_sf(
  xlim = c(xmid - x_half, xmid + x_half),
  ylim = c(ymid - y_half, ymid + y_half),
  expand = FALSE
) +
  labs(
    title = "Map of Poza Rica, Veracruz, México",
    subtitle = "Interactive floodplain layers",
    fill = "Flood Extent"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Floodplain (5yr)"   = cols[5],
      "Floodplain (10yr)"  = cols[4],
      "Floodplain (25yr)"  = cols[3],
      "Floodplain (50yr)"  = cols[2],
      "Floodplain (100yr)" = cols[1]
    ),
    breaks = c(
      "Floodplain (5yr)",
      "Floodplain (10yr)",
      "Floodplain (25yr)",
      "Floodplain (50yr)",
      "Floodplain (100yr)"
    )
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )

# -----------------------------
# Convert to plotly
# -----------------------------
g <- ggplotly(map)

# Hide floodplains initially
for (i in seq_along(g$x$data)) {
  nm <- g$x$data[[i]]$name
  if (!is.null(nm) && grepl("Floodplain", nm)) {
    g$x$data[[i]]$visible <- "legendonly"
  }
}

g



```

```{r}
#| eval: false

library(terra)
library(dplyr)
library(terra)

# compute slope and aspect from DEM
slope  <- terrain(dem, v = "slope", unit = "radians")
aspect <- terrain(dem, v = "aspect", unit = "radians")

# compute hillshade
hill <- shade(slope, aspect)

dem_df <- as.data.frame(dem, xy = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)
colnames(dem_df)[3] <- "elevation"

dem_df <- dem_df |>
  mutate(fct_elevation = cut(elevation, breaks = 3))

dem_df <- dem_df |>
  mutate(fct_elevation = cut(elevation, breaks = 5))

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(dem_df, aes(x = fct_elevation)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Elevation",
    x = "Elevation Bin",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

hill_df <- as.data.frame(hill, xy = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)
colnames(hill_df)[3] <- "hillshade"

ggplot() +
  geom_raster(
    data = hill_df,
    aes(x = x, y = y, alpha = hillshade)
  ) +
  scale_alpha(range = c(0.15, 0.65), guide = "none") +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_void()



```

```{r}
#| eval: false

slope  <- terrain(dem, v = "slope", unit = "radians")
aspect <- terrain(dem, v = "aspect", unit = "radians")
hill   <- shade(slope, aspect)


hill_df <- as.data.frame(hill, xy = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)
colnames(hill_df)[3] <- "hillshade"


map <- ggplot() +
  geom_raster(
    data = hill_df,
    aes(x = x, y = y, alpha = hillshade)
  ) +
  scale_alpha(range = c(0.15, 0.65), guide = "none") +
  coord_equal() + # prevents distortion
  theme_void()




ggplotly(map)
```

```{r}
#| eval: false

map <- ggplot() +
  # hillshade background
  geom_raster(
    data = hill_df,
    aes(x = x, y = y, fill = hillshade)
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "gray20", high = "gray95", guide = "none") +

  # river
  geom_sf(data = river, color = "blue", linewidth = 0.4) +

  # floodplain
  geom_sf(data = floodplain_100yr, fill = "red", alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +

  # buildings
  #geom_sf(data = buildings, color = "black", size = 0.1) +

  coord_sf() +
  theme_void() +
  labs(title = "Flood Exposure Map")

ggplotly(map)
```

Sources:

<https://carpentry.library.ucsb.edu/2022-08-09-ucsb-r-geospatial/02-raster-plot/>
